textByHash.5d1e7e29171bfe5dc649d669237c394a879c3b68: You, you're the human that killed Tyras. Arianwyn told us about you.
textByHash.249f6ddd5764788117f192f47cdf15c9ee5b5cb3: Why should we? All you human do is take and destroy with no care for others. We don't need your help. You've done enough damage.
textByHash.a1d50c3a8f6eb7390d49f6db5fdbe4a3ff87fd93: Maybe, but it's not a risk worth taking. It was the acts of humans that led my grantmother's death. You will bring similar ruin to us.
textByHash.f8bbf90a2058733ec9b0f6c0830c658c6d51cb62: Glarial, wife to King Baxtorian. At one time my grandparents ruled over a great elven kingdom. But your kind put an end to that.
textByHash.31e7b0917283c2c076c632b3a38dd20da9177667: You... you moved her into the waterfall? That... that's the perfect place for her to be.
textByHash.fa3897b6532fe03268c3d137cbba8861ffdc9514: Maybe... Very well human, we will take you to Arianwyn. But first, there is something you must do. If my grandmother has been moved, then her new tomb must be consecrated. Do this, and you will earn my trust.
textByHash.0a0fafcc242c221736fc31c4af5770647aeed9be: I don't know the consecration process myself. You should speak to Eluned. She is wise in the ways of the ritual.
textByHash.ba34e09736646d6e20e56879485e885363bdbe03: If you wish to see Arianwyn, this is the only way. Come find us if you change your mind.
textByHash.a40b538b8300abf7ffd6bf86baf54b52e1fe8eef: Good day. Have you spoken to Eluned yet?
textByHash.d3260d4693a920f73011daf0f8b74bd478f83768: Well I wish I could help but my kind have no place in the east any more.
textByHash.4df6970758ddaf2d0bfb6557ba5438ca5afb4bbc: Good, you should go and show it to her.
textByHash.fa794884520205b47540e3498d5592d23efc9366: Good day. I hear you managed to retrieve the crystal seed.
textByHash.e3372239a987b3084e86ef78dcd7a04792987924: This is good to hear. It put me at ease to know she is in my grandfather's home. I will rest even easier once it has been consecrated.
textByHash.51066093ca3b029f74a64b666191704901e455d7: You have returned! Is it done?
textByHash.b2b80bfa971148e3a4a57e70ddb5eaa43fb99dcd: Thank you, I admit I was wrong about you. You have showed care and compassion that I didn't think possible for a human. As promised, we will take you to Arianwyn. But first, a reward for you.
textByHash.b2d28d483149ea4d879233d485271517af7d914c: We elves use equipment formed out of crystal itself, far superior to your human weapons. However, the crystal does degrade with use and will eventually revert to seed. Both the shield and bow I am carrying have been used, so are not at their full potential. They should still be quite useful though.
textByHash.ce43be893d7b280c8d2394190b1252cad607b83b: All elven crystal starts as a seed. It can then be sung into various shapes through the power of the voice. Eventually, it reverts back to seed, ready for the next song. Keep an eye out for an elf named Ilfeen as you wander the forest. She can chant crystal seeds into new items for you.
textByHash.ab971ebf08762c570801197a2769e49b29d7016c: Anyway, I'm sure you will learn more about crystal seeds as you spend time with our kind. For now though, which one of these creations would you like?
textByHash.26035bb2b9f1409f0906274efa636e91bc7e11ec: All right. If you ever need a replacement I can sell you another. Remember, the better the condition of crystal equipment, the more potential it has.
textByHash.7cfbe7b7e9228c9db465cbcab63bf29d73468ef6: Very well. Come find me when you are ready.
textByHash.bf523365855defe838975b34361d019500cc10da: Now, let us know when you are ready to be taken to Arianwyn.
textByHash.860653136f5ed08ea3d8eb2472cc495a149fb0ab: Hello there. Are you ready to be taken to Arianwyn?
textByHash.640cbdd155b8061c3c84af9871779988f0ee36ea: Sorry friend, but I don't actually think you are.
textByHash.f2bb93dbe17b05f212a1d0589f84d68fe5ef4ce7: Very well. Arianwyn will be in Lletya, Eluned can take you there.
textByHash.416b6150c38a9e8db29448913ce184811bdf8b38: Well sorry, but you'll have to get 900,000 coins. Oh well... See you later.
textByHash.5e23a27853116d58dd129b2351eadc9dc32d5d49: Well sorry, but you don't have space to hold it.
textByHash.f500b9fca19f73318bc8cf17961a6f22cc4fa6fe: They're 900,000 coins each for new ones.
textByHash.a3523fcc1814b5951563e05c2eecb27b9e22b9fd: Sorry but they are very expensive to make. But it's your decision.
textByHash.e04580ab9b8e0c57321ffa22f7a2f6ff3595e480: Sloppy bearded mechanics wouldn't know decent craftsmanship if it bit them on the ankle! Besides, they are far too bulky.
textByHash.fd587d431a130d3619ca35b21c4bc1ea5e672257: Erm, are you trying to be funny?
textByHash.e8faa5d1b413ccabc8d5b5811abe07d2b54ff0fd: Well sorry, but you'll have to get 750,000 coins. Oh well... See you later.
textByHash.975cb8604b3c63a5b965be2ae208ec0d12c63a11: They're 750,000 coins each for new ones.
textByHash.b5229bb1caa2a7e3624bdcc92f44090cab05b52d: Ilfeen can chant them into new items for you. She'll be wandering the forest somewhere.
textByHash.f98b258666ffd090365102c6006ebc3274abffb0: No problem. Let us know when you are ready to be taken to Arianwyn.
textByHash.c69a46a1c5417f5154c118d8124c7a59c28e4513: Fair enough. Let us know when you are ready to be taken to Arianwyn.
textByHash.959640841764838b3c7722cc4656d7f90fbcc6a4: Good to see you . You should speak to Arianwyn when you're ready to begin.
textByHash.070e27be5658e8c98b28c91bb2524ef2fdf01068: You say that like it's a bad thing, Ysgawyn. Last time I checked, we didn't care about upsetting our foe.
textByHash.e98ddad6c51f0fb52d05734568d9287985b9dc0c: That's impossible. He must be lying.
textByHash.b7a1d6efdb79e851029d40f730c6cbb3048978b4: No. Prifddinas was lost to us. When Lord Iorwerth first took control of the city, my grandfather, Baxtorian, led an army to try and stop him.
textByHash.b697812027e786ec8570b4d6b2ac60f973f17b4a: When that failed, Baxtorian and the other clan elders made one final ditch attempt to save Prifddinas. They sung the song of inversion to revert the entire city to a crystal seed.
textByHash.2ecca0b9db0387b8a00b39b249552d6e85fb512f: While he was fighting Lord Iorwerth, invaders took control of our kingdom to the east. They kidnapped Glarial, Baxtorian's wife.
textByHash.0ff0a0c9ff92b4a438a6ced3431c5b3c88e4b3b3: Unable to find her, he sealed himself beneath the waterfall where they lived, never to be seen again.
textByHash.a541eb4146ac3573d37a41f166cb312bb734a8bb: It's not impossible I suppose. If anyone, it would have been him. Baxtorian was closer to Seren than any other elf, it's one of the reasons he was chosen to become king.
textByHash.b9b3b13224aafd5384a058e315e33ed1fa334097: Don't be so sure. I never knew my grandfather, but my mother always told me that he wasn't the type for taking chances. If he knew something important, he'd have made sure it wouldn't have been lost with him.
textByHash.91b53e5d7bcf82cc87d6df3ba0b5816be79a389d: Baxtorian kept everything of importance to him at his home within Baxtorian Falls. If we're going to find anything, it's probably there.
textByHash.0280caf7445d68da0b3fb3b0fe310be33f835821: Let go of your pride, Ysgawyn. has proven his worth to us.
textByHash.6bba8be6b38f4677e339fd3fd8c83ee083e4d5dc: Before her death, my mother passed down a book to me. It was written by my grandfather for his wife. You should take it with you. It might contain something of use.
textByHash.05f25931ef263cd03c4daed68442a169cb814e88: Not nothing. We now know that the seals can be destroyed, which means Lord Iorwerth could have spoken the truth.
textByHash.e4430458e30c90e97c85cf954786b4bff4d119ef: I propose we send in. They have proven to us time and time again that they have the skill needed for a challenge like this.
textByHash.0078fafaefaca2161187a6f53648caff5349be63: So he did break one of the seals. Did it hide anything notable?
textByHash.00062648c0a344a03d0dee0999d5388567f35635: I... I don't believe it. She cursed us? Our goddess cursed us?
textByHash.452c1fbc12edde7b04b58ddf87043a179055f5de: This can't be real... This is a dream... Tell me this isn't real!
textByHash.8a8e96c9cbbb3c28b18aa05cd383858c6375f7e9: That's not your call. Arianwyn is in charge here.
textByHash.3f5fad436c334b99347251a34366188de68903e1: If the orb isn't at Gorlah, Lord Iorwerth must have claimed it. It could be anywhere now.
textByHash.dc3b40219854e50bf76e3be4264d601b205dff23: No, you just serve yourself, don't you Ysgawyn?
textByHash.7a47eb8dc49c23afa1b5f5bd0d1c68d9e9732caa: The orb isn't enough for him to win though, he'll also need to break through the defences on the Temple of Light. He'll struggle to do that now he's lost Ardougne.
textByHash.cee8ad9b8a2c102682840b224faeebdf47137603: Sounds like we're done here. Let's get moving.
textByHash.8306b575825ec39ada0d2624a0aa12dd993d2b41: We do not barter with your kind!
textByHash.bc8f22868e54ffa6905505792fe44357c2de62db: You're a monster! Surely you don't mean to side with this barbarian, Arianwyn.
textByHash.77248fd4cfb4cd867030cf6e013b21673749d178: He wasn't a friend, to any of us. Everything he cared about he lost a long time ago.
textByHash.2aa442bf26e493f074e83fb6b72e5b4758706c8d: But he didn't give up. To the very end, he fought for a cause he believed in, our cause.
textByHash.7121cdbf8a7bde3704c555641c11fe6bad7daafe: And now he's gone. Along with the orb and that traitor, Arianwyn. Lord Iorwerth has everything he needs now. He'll be preparing to move on the temple as we speak.
textByHash.9c71d68743461934c1870c240930cae0df94ea60: Oh I don't intend to give up, but make no mistake, this is a fight we're unlikely to win.
textByHash.e0b00625afc3dabed17054e22de09df49e8b739a: Maybe, but we have to try. What else is there?
textByHash.ef8b0e7c260bdd7495296f4b89b34b87cf33ca0c: Well Lord Iorwerth seemed to think you were no stranger to that. Why did he think that?
textByHash.3b80aa903cd6ef4d01cf2a628da048e9c6b0308a: I wouldn't trust Lord Iorwerth if my life depended on it. I know a liar when I see one though, and I can tell you're not being honest about something.
textByHash.0caae37605ed6e0a8111be56b4dd53b5fcad0c5d: You... you killed them? You killed them all?
textByHash.f5036113e0da8d74d81c33c5db738ceb995475e3: You think I don't understand love? I knew love, for Eirlys! My mother! Your daughter! She died in your war, while you hid away!
textByHash.cfd3f3d2efe3377f8fcdff08a4acc2d9eb191813: Lord Iorwerth was right! You're just a coward!
textByHash.1ca2e4261235b94463c043be560afe8e26b3fdf6: The Underground Pass! I intend to fight for my people, even if their own king won't!
textByHash.fce249aa4c91a8eb1a96224923448c9277e13452: As good as can be, all things considered. I idolised my grandfather for years. They always say you should never meet your heroes though, now I see why.
textByHash.2b1167da6817f755b120313fcc3997c9b4657255: He is, he's the only family I have left. The rest of them died in his war. A war that will finally end today.
textByHash.325a6884faff2712a2e55c65c16befc552c34333: Maybe if we both survive, I'll find it in my heart to forgive him. But for now, I have to focus elsewhere.
textByHash.b52d26c00c6162e97302267a61133835791529f2: Good luck . I'll see you on the other side.
textByHash.0117ce52957398c34b8e2aefa24974ea339a1f44: Good to see you. Are you after some crystal equipment?
textByHash.3001d7bbfb09214de84fdc24ee3501cef76f7691: Ilfeen can chant them into new items for you.
